Required outputs
1. Role mapping matrix:
- Light semantic role
- Current dark mapping
- Candidate PoC dark mapping
- Rationale per row

2. Candidate anchor set for Slate behavior:
- 1100, 1000, 900, 200, 50 equivalents
- Intent notes for layer separation, readability, and elevation coherence

3. Validation report:
- WCAG AA pass/fail per required pairing
- APCA observations as secondary quality signal

Accessibility policy (mandatory final gate)
- WCAG 2.1 AA text:
1. Normal text at least 4.5:1
2. Large text at least 3:1
- Non-text UI boundaries/states where applicable at least 3:1
- Any failed AA check is an automatic fail for that candidate.

Quality policy (secondary)
- Run APCA checks on key text/background pairs after AA is satisfied.
- Use APCA to refine dark-mode readability and reduce visual fatigue.

Success criteria
1. Layering
- Clear perceptual separation between page 1100, container 1000, and menu 900 without over-carding.

2. Readability
- Long-form default text is comfortable on both page and container.
- Heading and deemphasized tiers are distinct and intentional.

3. Interactive and link text
- Hyperlink default, hover, active, focus, and visited behavior is defined where applicable.
- Interactive text is clearly identifiable without relying on color alone.
- Underline or equivalent affordance behavior is consistent.
- Link/interactive states remain distinguishable on page, container, and menu layers.

4. Structural clarity
- Borders/dividers support structure without visual noise.

Constraints
- Do not rewrite production tokens in this pass.
- Do not change non-Slate families in Phase 1.
- Keep compatibility with existing token-set wiring.

Final deliverable format
1. Concise summary
2. Mapping matrix
3. Candidate anchors
4. WCAG AA results table
5. APCA observations
6. Risks and open questions
7. Recommended next iteration steps

I’m implementing that merged prompt directly in the sandbox now: I’ll add a dedicated Dark PoC mode, namespaced PoC styles, and a built-in report section with mapping matrix, candidate anchors, WCAG/APCA outputs, and success-criteria checks.I’m gathering the current sandbox structure from the main TS and CSS files first so the PoC mode slots in cleanly without disturbing existing tabs or baseline behavior.
I’ve confirmed the sandbox already has the right navigation scaffolding; next I’m patching in a new Dark PoC mode and a report panel without touching production token files.
I’m now applying the implementation patch: adding a Dark PoC mode, a dedicated report page, and generated WCAG/APCA validation tables directly in the sandbox UI.

data.content:
Implemented the merged prompt in the sandbox with an isolated Dark PoC mode and report workflow.

What I changed
1. Added a dedicated Dark PoC mode to layering controls and state handling in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L447), with mode parsing/wiring updates in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L31), [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L52), and [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L861).
2. Added a dedicated Dark PoC report page/tab in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L454) and [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L727).
3. Implemented generated PoC report content in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L260):
- Role mapping matrix (light semantic -> current dark -> candidate dark + rationale)
- Candidate anchor set (1100/1000/900/200/50 equivalents)
- WCAG AA table with computed contrast ratios and pass/fail
- APCA secondary observations (qualitative note per pair)
- Risks and next steps
4. Added namespaced Dark PoC styling lane in [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css#L158).
5. Added PoC report/table/chip/link-state styles and interactive-link affordance rules in [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css#L887), including distinct default/hover/active/focus/visited visuals and underline behavior.

Validation
1. Build completed successfully with your repo’s existing token-reference warnings (same class of warnings as before).
2. No new editor errors in the changed files:
- [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts)
- [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css)

If you want, next I can extend this same PoC report structure to Phase 2 families (status + data viz) while keeping the same AA-first gate.
this looks good, but I would LOVE the dark mode PoC to have a light mode baseline, ex: you can toggle between light and dark to see token translation. how could we best do this?

You can absolutely add that, and the cleanest approach is to reuse the existing evaluation theme pattern and extend it into the Dark PoC report so each candidate token shows both light baseline and dark translation side-by-side. I’ll quickly pull exact hook points so I can give you a precise implementation plan.

Best approach: make the Dark PoC report a dual-mode translator view, not just a dark-only report.

What I’d do
1. Reuse your existing theme-toggle plumbing, instead of creating new state.
- You already have light/dark controls in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L662).
- You already have the setter in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L806).
- Wire the Dark PoC report rendering in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L260) to read that same `data-eval-theme`.

2. Add a true “baseline vs translation” section to the report.
- In [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L260), define two anchor maps:
  - `lightBaseline` (sourced from light semantic intent)
  - `darkCandidate` (your PoC values)
- Render both in one row per role: Light baseline -> Dark translation.
- Keep current matrix columns, but add 2 columns: “Light baseline hex” and “Dark translated hex”.

3. Add a report-level mode switch UX.
- Put a small toggle at top of the Dark PoC report in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L727):
  - `View: Light baseline | Dark translation | Side-by-side`
- `Side-by-side` is usually best for design review and avoids context switching fatigue.

4. Expand validation to compare both modes.
- In [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L260), run WCAG checks for:
  - Light pairs
  - Dark pairs
- Show “AA pass both modes” as the gate for translated roles.
- Keep APCA note as dark-quality secondary signal.

5. Scope styles so it stays isolated.
- Keep PoC-only styling in your existing lane at [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css#L158).
- Add light-baseline fixture variables under `.local-layer-dark-poc` in [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css#L875), toggled by `body[data-eval-theme='light']` and `body[data-eval-theme='dark']`.

Why this is the best fit here
- Minimal new state.
- Clear token translation story for stakeholders.
- Maintains your AA-first policy while still leveraging APCA for dark tuning.
- No production token mutation.

If you want, I can implement this next as a focused patch: “Dark PoC baseline toggle + side-by-side translation table + dual-mode WCAG results.”
ok

I’m ready to implement it, but this chat is currently in read-only Ask mode, so I can’t apply file edits from here.

If you switch to Edit/Agent mode, I’ll patch it immediately in:
- [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts)
- [src/dev/extra-styles.css](src/dev/extra-styles.css)

Planned implementation on apply:
1. Add a Dark PoC report view toggle: Light baseline, Dark translation, Side-by-side.
2. Reuse existing eval theme state (`data-eval-theme`) so no duplicate state machine.
3. Expand the mapping matrix to include both light baseline hex and dark translated hex.
4. Compute WCAG rows for both modes and add a “passes in both modes” column.
5. Keep APCA as dark-only secondary quality note.
6. Add isolated PoC CSS rules for light/dark preview cards and link-state fixtures.

If you want, I can also provide a ready-to-paste diff next while you stay in Ask mode.
do your thing

I’m implementing it now: I’ll wire the Dark PoC report to the existing light/dark eval theme state, add a view toggle (light, dark, side-by-side), and expand the matrix plus WCAG table to show token translation across modes. I’m pulling the exact report block to patch safely.
I found the exact report generator; I’m now patching it to include dual-mode (light baseline + dark translation) data, a report view toggle, and combined WCAG pass logic across both modes.

How to use it

- Light baseline: inspect source intent tokens.
- Dark translation: inspect translated dark targets.
- Side-by-side: review translation parity and AA compliance together.

You can find them in two places:

1. In the running sandbox UI

i was hoping to have a side by side light PoC of actual UI changing between light and dark. What do you think about simply naming this page "OKLCH PoC" and introducing a toggle swithc between light and dark themes?

Yes, I think that is the right move.

Renaming this to “OKLCH PoC” and making the core experience a real light/dark UI toggle will be clearer and more useful than a report-first page. It shifts the page from “documentation” to “design validation,” which sounds like what you want now.

Recommended direction
1. Rename the page label from Dark PoC report to OKLCH PoC in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L454).
2. Keep one PoC fixture UI, but add a theme switch at the top (Light/Dark, optional Side-by-side) in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L357).
3. Drive actual UI theme changes through mode classes (light vs dark), not only table data, so buttons/inputs/links visibly change.
4. Keep WCAG/APCA tables, but position them as diagnostics below the live fixture, not the main content.

Implementation nuance
- Right now body is initialized with dark mode in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L3).  
- For a true UI switch, toggle theme classes (or scope them to a PoC wrapper) so SKY UI tokens actually re-resolve in both modes.

Why this is better
- Immediate visual proof of token translation quality.
- Easier stakeholder review.
- Keeps the PoC focused on behavior, not just metrics.

are the pills that say "WCAG AA gate (both modes): FAIL" actually reporting status?
I’m checking the exact status-pill logic in the PoC report generator so I can confirm whether those FAIL labels are live-calculated or static text.

Short answer: yes, they are computed, not hardcoded text.

What is happening now:
1. The FAIL/PASS pills are generated from calculated contrast checks in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L322), [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L344), and [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L361).
2. Ratios are computed by the WCAG function in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L281).
3. The inputs come from the fixed check list in [src/dev/main.ts](src/dev/main.ts#L197).

Important nuance:
1. They are reporting status against the predefined light/dark PoC hex pairs, not sampling the live rendered CSS values.
2. They are also not re-derived from whatever you toggle visually; they are based on the static check data baked into that report generation.

If you want, I can switch this to true live reporting next by reading the current computed style values from the active PoC UI elements and recalculating the pills on each theme toggle.
